CSC 111.title: Introduction to Computers and Basic Programming
CSC 111.description: Introduction to computer programming with particular emphasis on small systems through programming in the BASIC language.

CSC 116.title: Introduction to Computer Hardware and Tools
CSC 116.description: Organization of sequential, digital machine: CPU, I/O, storage, communications devices. Functions of operating systems, translators, editors, peripheral control utilities.

CSC 255.title: Dynamic Web Programming
CSC 255.description: Introduces fundamental structures and logical principles that form the foundation of computer science. Students write programs to deepen their understanding.

CSC 301.title: Computers And Society
CSC 301.description: Ethical, legal, psychological, economic, and theoretical implications and limitations of the uses of digital computers. Oral and written presentations required.

CTC 310.title: Software Project Management
CTC 310.description: Focus on critical aspects of project management, including software project management concepts, roadmap, and effective software project management approaches.

CTC 316.title: Operating Systems and Networks Support
CTC 316.description: Introduction to OS and networking support. Topics include risk management, mission assurance, enterprise management tools, disaster recovery, and business continuity.

CTC 328.title: Computer Forensics and Investigation
CTC 328.description: Methods to properly conduct a computer forensics investigation, ethics, and IACIS certification objectives.

CTC 362.title: Communication Systems Security
CTC 362.description: Foundation for understanding key issues associated with protecting information assets, security incidents, and designing a consistent information security system.

CTC 428.title: Operating Systems Security
CTC 428.description: In-depth look at operating system security concepts and techniques, examining theoretical concepts and practical hands-on operating system security techniques.

CTC 452.title: Network Security and Hacking Prevention
CTC 452.description: In-depth look at network defense concepts and techniques, examining theoretical networking concepts and practical network defense techniques and strategies.
CTC 452.notes: Elective — choose 2 of 5; Consent of instructor required
